messages.content: How many experts does the Llama 4 Maverick model have?

{"query": "Llama 4 Maverick model number of experts", "top_k": [{"url": "https://www.theregister.com/2025/04/07/llama_4_debuts/", "title": "Meta debuts its first 'mixture of experts' models from the Llama 4 herd", "content": "Llama 4 Maverick, is larger with 128 experts totaling 402 billion parameters, though like Scout only 17 billion are active for any given query.", "score": 0.9999676, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://www.ibm.com/new/announcements/meta-llama-4-maverick-and-llama-4-scout-now-available-in-watsonx-ai", "title": "Meta Llama 4 Maverick and Llama 4 Scout now available in watsonx ...", "content": "# Meta Llama 4 Maverick and Llama 4 Scout now available in watsonx.ai. **IBM is excited to announce the addition of Meta\u2019s latest generation of open models, Llama 4, to** **watsonx.ai****.** Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, the first mixture of experts (MoE) models released by Meta, provide frontier multimodal performance, high speeds, low cost, and industry leading context length. With the introduction of these latest offerings from Meta, IBM now supports a total of 13 Meta models in the expansive library of \u00a0foundation models available in watsonx.ai. Trained on 40 trillion tokens of data, Llama 4 Scout offers performance rivalling or exceeding that of models with significantly larger active parameter counts while keeping costs and latency low. ## Llama 4 models on IBM watsonx. Developers and businesses can select their preferred Llama 4 model from the extensive catalog of foundation models on IBM watsonx.ai, then fine-tune, distill and deploy it across cloud, on-premises, or edge environments of their choice. Start using Llama 4 models on watsonx.ai today. Start using Llama 4 models on watsonx.ai today.", "score": 0.99992096, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-accelerates-inference-on-meta-llama-4-scout-and-maverick/", "title": "NVIDIA Accelerates Inference on Meta Llama 4 Scout and Maverick", "content": "* The new Llama 4 models, Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, are natively multimodal and multilingual, using a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture and accelerated by NVIDIA open-source software. * Llama 4 Scout is a 109B-parameter model optimized for a single NVIDIA H100 GPU, while Llama 4 Maverick is a 400B-parameter model that delivers high-performance image and text understanding. Llama 4 Scout is a 109B-parameter model, 17B active per token, with a configuration of 16 experts boasting a 10M context-length window, and optimized and quantized to int4 for a single NVIDIA H100 GPU. NVIDIA optimized both Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick models for NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM. TensorRT-LLM is an open-source library used to accelerate LLM inference performance for the latest foundation models on NVIDIA GPUs. You can use TensorRT Model Optimizer, a library that can refactor bfloat16 models with the latest algorithmic model optimizations and quantization techniques, to accelerate inference with the Blackwell FP4 Tensorcore performance without impacting model accuracy.", "score": 0.9994429, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://bdtechtalks.com/2025/04/06/meta-llama-4/", "title": "What to know about Meta's Llama 4 model family - TechTalks", "content": "Home Blog What to know about Meta\u2019s Llama 4 model family. # What to know about Meta\u2019s Llama 4 model family. Meta has just released Llama 4, its most powerful family of open-weight large language models (LLMs). Llama 4 is a family of LLMs, which Meta refers to as a \u201cherd of models.\u201d It comes in three sizes: Behemoth (2 trillion parameters), Maverick (400 billion parameters), and Scout (109 billion parameters). Meta also used Llama 4 Behemoth as a \u201cteacher\u201d model, which means they use its outputs to train smaller models (also called \u201cmodel distillation\u201d). According to the figures shared by Meta, the Llama 4 models match or surpass their open and closed counterparts on key benchmarks. It is worth noting that Meta did not include comparisons to leading reasoning models such as OpenAI o1 and o3 or Google Gemini 2.5 Pro. It also left out benchmarks where other models such as DeepSeek V3.1 outperform Llama 4, as some users have pointed out on social media.", "score": 0.9979966, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://medium.com/@tahirbalarabe2/metas-new-llama-4-s-moe-architecture-makes-ai-faster-cheaper-635339e51e10", "title": "Meta's New Llama 4's MoE Architecture Makes AI Faster & Cheaper", "content": "Llama 4 Maverick also uses 17 billion active parameters but has 128 experts, with 400 billion total parameters stored (but not all used at once)", "score": 0.9976404, "raw_content": null}]}
